raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Federal Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) has modified their Housing First guidelines to include drug free recovery housing as a component.",
    "California law has not been updated to reflect the modified HUD guidelines regarding drug free recovery housing.",
    "Drug free recovery housing is a widely proven and critical tool in confronting addiction.",
    "In San Francisco, 68% of overdoses in the last year happened at a fixed address, many of which were harm reduction facilities.",
    "Many individuals seeking recovery do not want to live next to others who are still using drugs.",
    "Drug free recovery housing allows a community of people ready to be fully sober to help keep each other accountable.",
    "People living in drug free housing are provided support needed to avoid falling back into drug use or homelessness.",
    "AB 2479 would bring California into compliance with federal best practices by making recovery housing an option for those who need it.",
    "AB 2479 allows for up to 25% of state funding to go towards drug free housing.",
    "Residents of drug free housing who relapse do not automatically get evicted, but instead receive support with detoxification programs and intensive peer-to-peer support."
  ]
}
```
